job_description:
Job Summary: As a member of our client’s Salesforce Applications team, you will assist in building scalable and efficient healthcare data integration and data processing services. You will work on solutions that deploy front-end services as Salesforce application plugins and back-end services using Microsoft’s Azure public cloud infrastructure. The ideal candidate will have a passion for coding, quality, and security, and be eager to learn and grow in a fast-paced environment. Responsibilities: • Assist in designing, documenting, and implementing engineering solutions. • Participate in full stack coding including front-end Salesforce applications, back-end services in Azure, databases, cloud infrastructure, and test automation. • Help produce high-quality code by creating and executing test plans, utilizing test automation, and participating in code reviews. • Follow Secure by Design development practices. • Assist in creating threat models. • Support Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA). • Analyze engineering challenges and help develop solutions. • Participate in code reviews for quality, security, and maintainability.


Will not be considered without the following experience:

  1. Bachelor's Degree
  2. 1 year of experience as a software developer with NO SALESFORCE BACKGROUND is completely okay, Candidates will be trained in Salesforce via a training program!
  3. Software Development/Engineering experience is a must, 1+ years of experience in a full time role preferred or solid internship experience with strong determination/motivation.
  4. Strong preference for the C#/.Net stack. Python or Java okay but must be a strong developer and learn quickly.
  5. Azure exposure but AWS or GCP fine as well.

Position Summary:

  1. Triage, resolve, escalate, and track incoming IT/IS support requests.
  2. Provide technical and administrative support for systems and banking applications.
  3. Handle system analysis, provisioning, upgrades, and deployment.
  4. Collaborate with IT/IS team and bank staff to ensure network security and efficiency.

Essential Duties:

  1. Adhere to SLA requirements – Provide timely, accurate feedback and updates to users.
  2. Helpdesk Management – Filter, prioritize, and troubleshoot IT support tickets.
  3. Tech Support – Provide first/second-level troubleshooting (password resets, hardware/software issues).
  4. Documentation – Record and track problem-solving processes and resolutions.
  5. User Management – Set up accounts, permissions, passwords, and deploy new workstations.
  6. Training & Support – Conduct IT orientation, create help guides, and train staff on systems.
  7. Project Assistance – Research, implement, and maintain system standards.
  8. Customer Service – Uphold the Bank’s Mission, Vision, and Core Values.
  9. Other Duties – Perform additional tasks as assigned.

Qualifications & Skills:

  1. Education: Bachelor’s in IT or equivalent experience.
  2. Experience: 3+ years in customer service; IT helpdesk & banking experience preferred.
  3. Communication: Ability to simplify complex technical issues for users.
  4. Problem-Solving: Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and prioritization skills.
  5. Multitasking: Work efficiently in a fast-paced environment with minimal supervision.
  6. Attendance: Regular, reliable, and timely presence required.

Physical Requirements:

  1. Lift/move 20-30 lbs occasionally.
  2. Vision: Close, distance, color, and peripheral vision required.
  3. Frequent sitting, hand use, speaking, and hearing.
  4. Occasional reaching, stooping, bending, kneeling, and crouching.

Overview:

T-Mobile is seeking a QA Analyst with 1–3 years of manual testing experience for a long-term contract opportunity supporting a QA team within the enterprise-level Telecommunications industry. This is a fully remote position, but candidates must be able to work Pacific Standard Time (PST) hours. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, collaborative, and eager to grow within a supportive QA team.

What You’ll Do:

  1. Participate in requirement reviews and contribute to test planning discussions
  2. Document and execute test cases based on functional requirements and user stories

  4. Perform regression, functional, and exploratory testing
  5. Collaborate with developers, QA team members, and project managers to ensure quality deliverables

  1. 1–3 years of manual QA experience, preferably in Agile/Scrum environments

  5. Familiarity with QA methodologies and the software development lifecycle (SDLC)
  6. Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote setting
  7. Located in the U.S. and available to work full PST business hours

Nice to Have:

Job Overview:
T-Mobile Cloud is looking for a Member of Technical Staff (MTS) Hardware Engineer who will oversee the hardware design and integration of server racks from Original Design Manufacturers (ODMs). This position will work closely with our internal customers to understand their workloads and applications needs to provide a reliable hardware server solution. This role will be the technical decision maker for product management, system architecture, and engineering for the hardware design of the Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning (AI/ML), networking, compute, and storage rack infrastructure.
This experienced MTS Hardware Engineer is a visionary who possesses the technological acumen needed to translate the vision into a roadmap and communicate how the roadmap translates into product development. The applicant will have experience in delivering new product introduction (NPI) and product launches.
The candidate for this position will possess robust technical skills and is adept at navigating a rapidly evolving environment characterized by substantial ambiguity. The candidate will bring extensive experience in the enterprise hardware rack and server architecture, coupled with a proven track record of collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver exceptional reliability at an optimal cost.
The position will own the end-to-end hardware design strategy, in-rack power, and thermal, and safety requirements. They make recommendations for new products and overall hardware architecture approach by partnering with senior level engineers that will run their workloads and applications on the cloud hardware.
This position will lead the transformation of our on-premises rack and server infrastructure, driving innovation and delivering cutting-edge solutions to meet the evolving needs of our customers and the telecommunications industry.

Job Responsibilities:

Responsibilities:
• Lead the technical design and implementation of large-scale, cross-platform cloud hardware solutions to optimize infrastructure performance and reliability.
• Develop and drive the technology roadmap for cloud hardware, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and industry best practices.
• Collaborate with senior engineers and cross-functional teams to evaluate and integrate new hardware products and architectural approaches.
• Mentor and guide engineers in technical development, fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement.
• Conduct signal integrity analysis, and manage CPU, memory, peripheral interfaces, and device firmware interactions to ensure robust system performance.
• Oversee the quality and reliability of third-party hardware suppliers, working closely with the supply chain to maintain high standards.
• Analyze competitive market and industry trends to identify opportunities for innovation and strategic advantage.
• Serve as the technical spokesperson for rack and server engineering, representing T-Mobile's technical capabilities.

Qualifications:
• 7-10 years related work experience (Required)
• 5+ years developing cloud server and rack hardware solutions (Required)
• 5+ years as lead technologist, architect engineer in the hardware space for a major technology, component, product, product line
• Experience with memory, CPU, signal integrity, peripheral device integration, hardware monitoring sensors, and device firmware interaction (Required)
• Experience as a lead engineer for complex electronic systems (Required)
• Experience driving the technology roadmap and quality of third-party suppliers and working with supply chain (Required)
• Experience with mentoring engineers on technical development (Required)
• Experience with in-rack power, cooling, and thermal solutions (fans, heatsinks, liquid cooling, power supply units, inverters, and batteries) (Preferred)
• Experience with statistical analysis and modeling for reliability, performance, and cost (Preferred)
• Experience Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA) board layout optimization (Preferred)
• Experience in driving hardware reliability through design enhancement and failure mode analysis (Preferred)
• Experience with data center deployments of compute related infrastructure (Preferred)
• Experience with the hardware development life cycle management (part library management, PLM); and bill of material (BOM) part numbering management (Preferred)

Education:
• Bachelor's Degree of Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, a related subject area, or equivalent industry experience (Required)
• Masters/PhD/Advanced Degree of Engineering, Computer Science, or a related subject area (Preferred)

At JazzHR, strong leadership drives success. We're seeking ambitious individuals to join us as an Entry Level Management Trainee.

This role offers hands-on experience, leadership skill development, and growth within a dynamic environment.

We designed this position for entry-level candidates who want to learn every aspect of managing a successful business and deepen their understanding of sales.

With comprehensive training, our Entry Level Management Trainee will be prepared to take on leadership roles and make a significant impact in our organization while having a larger understanding of sales, customer service, and business interactions.
Entry Level Management Trainee Responsibilities
• Learn and master the fundamentals of business, including customer service, driving sales, and management.
• Collaborate with team leaders to implement sales strategies that drive business and customer satisfaction.
• Assist in managing daily sales operations and ensuring smooth workflow across departments.
• Develop strong relationships with clients and customers to build trust and loyalty.
• Analyze performance and sales metrics and provide recommendations for process improvements.
• Participate in leadership training sessions to enhance your management capabilities.
• Take on increasing responsibilities with the goal of advancing to a leadership role.
Requirements
• Strong interest in business, management, and leadership development.
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
• A proactive approach to problem-solving and decision-making.
• The ability to thrive in a fast-paced, goal-oriented environment.

• A collaborative mindset and eagerness to work as part of a team.
• Previous experience in sales, customer service, or leadership roles is a plus, but not required.

Take the first step toward a rewarding career in business management and sales development.

  6. Manage and forecast pipeline activity with accuracy

  2. Proven track record of meeting or exceeding quota in a regional sales role
  3. Strong understanding of the healthcare provider ecosystem and credentialing workflows

  2. Experience selling to credentialing or compliance teams

job_title: Customer Relationship Management UX Specialist (Mid-Level or Senior)
job_description:
Job Description

At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.

The Boeing Company is currently seeking a Customer Relationship Management UX Specialist (Mid-Level or Senior) to join the team in Seattle, WA or Renton, WA.

As a CRM UX Specialist, you will spearhead user-centered design initiatives for our enterprise CRM platforms, primarily focusing on Salesforce and related tools. Your expertise will be instrumental in mapping user journeys, creating intuitive interfaces, and streamlining workflows for sales, marketing, service, and support teams. By conducting user research, usability testing, and accessibility reviews, you will continuously refine designs to meet user needs. Collaborating closely with product managers, developers, and business stakeholders, you will ensure our CRM solutions are engaging, accessible, and aligned with Boeing’s high standards. This pivotal role supports our digital transformation by delivering seamless, data-driven experiences across our CRM ecosystem.

Position Responsibilities:
• Lead user research activities—including interviews, surveys, and usability tests—to gather insights that drive design improvements
• Conduct accessibility assessments and usability evaluations to ensure CRM interfaces meet WCAG standards
• Develop comprehensive user flows and journey maps for diverse CRM personas, identifying pain points and designing targeted solutions
• Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs using tools like Figma and Adobe XD to develop intuitive, user-centric interfaces
• Collaborate with product managers, stakeholders, and technical teams to define requirements, prioritize features, and ensure smooth integration of UX solutions.
• Document UX strategies, design guidelines, and maintain a reusable design system to ensure consistency across platforms
• Apply responsive and accessible design standards, delivering optimal desktop and mobile experiences
• Partner with Information Technology (IT) and CRM administrators to support data flow, third-party integrations, and custom modules, ensuring user-friendly data interactions
• Mentor junior designers and promote UX best practices within cross-functional teams, fostering a user-centric culture
• Lead UX strategy and prototyping efforts across Salesforce clouds, supporting end-to-end release cycles, including post-deployment validation
• Utilize tools such as Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, and Copado for design, prototyping, and release readiness reviews
• Ensure designs align with Boeing’s branding, UI standards, and security protocols, integrating data security and user access considerations

Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience):
• 3+ years of experience designing enterprise web or mobile applications, especially CRM systems (preferably Salesforce)
• 3+ years of experience with Salesforce Lightning, Flows, and CRM-specific UX workflows
• 3+ years of experience in user research, usability testing, and A/B testing methodologies
• 3+ years of experience with design and prototyping tools such as Figma, Adobe XD, or similar
• Experience working within Agile environments, utilizing tools like Azure DevOps, JIRA, etc.
• Experience with CI/CD pipelines and release management tools like Copado

Preferred Qualifications (Desired Skills/Experience):
• 5 or more years' related work experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience Salesforce UX Designer Certification
• Salesforce Administrator Certification
• Experience with Salesforce Experience Cloud (Communities), Mobile Publisher, or customized Lightning experiences
• Experience in data architecture, SSO integration, and security role mapping.
• Experience in regulated enterprise environments such as aerospace, manufacturing, or defense
• Experience with user-centered design solutions for complex business applications
• Experience in continuous improvement or lean workshops

Drug Free Workplace:

Boeing is a Drug Free Workplace where post offer applicants and employees are subject to testing for marijuana, cocaine, opioids, amphetamines, PCP, and alcohol when criteria is met as outlined in our policies.

Pay & Benefits:

At Boeing, we strive to deliver a Total Rewards package that will attract, engage and retain the top talent. Elements of the Total Rewards package include competitive base pay and variable compensation opportunities.

job_title: Remote Senior UX/UI Designer - SaaS Platform
job_description:
Our client, a rapidly growing Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company, is searching for a talented and experienced Remote Senior UX/UI Designer to lead the user experience and interface design for their innovative platform. This is a fully remote role, offering flexibility and the chance to work with a forward-thinking product team.

As a Senior UX/UI Designer, you will be responsible for translating complex user needs and business requirements into intuitive, engaging, and aesthetically pleasing digital experiences. Your core duties will include conducting user research, creating user personas, developing user journey maps, and designing wireframes, mockups, and interactive prototypes. You will play a key role in defining the overall visual design language and interaction patterns for the platform, ensuring consistency and usability across all touchpoints.

Key responsibilities encompass collaborating closely with product managers, engineers, and other designers to ideate and iterate on design solutions. You will conduct usability testing, gather user feedback, and incorporate insights to refine designs. The ability to articulate design decisions and rationale clearly to stakeholders is crucial. You will also contribute to the development and maintenance of design systems and style guides, ensuring scalability and coherence.

The ideal candidate will have a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Design, HCI, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. A minimum of 6 years of professional experience in UX/UI design, with a strong portfolio showcasing successful web and mobile application designs, is required. Expertise in design and prototyping tools such as Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, or InVision is essential. You should have a deep understanding of user-centered design principles, information architecture, and interaction design. Experience designing for complex SaaS applications or enterprise software is highly desirable. Familiarity with front-end development principles (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and accessibility standards (WCAG) is a significant plus. Strong communication, collaboration, and presentation skills are a must.

We are looking for a creative thinker with a keen eye for detail and a passion for creating exceptional user experiences. You should be adept at balancing user needs with business goals and technical constraints. This remote role requires self-discipline, excellent time management, and a proactive approach to problem-solving. Join us in shaping the future of our digital product.

Launch Your Career in Tech with Our Entry-Level Opportunities

Fine-tuned written and verbal communication skills are critical for both roles. Desired skills include NLP, text mining, Tableau, PowerBI, Databricks, and TensorFlow.

Data Center Technician-IT Technician (DCT) Level 1


The Data Center Technician Level 1 is responsible for supporting the physical infrastructure and operational uptime of a data center. This includes staging, installing, maintaining, and decommissioning hardware such as servers, racks, switches, and drives. The technician follows standardized protocols to perform startups, shutdowns, diagnostics, and repairs, ensuring systems meet performance and safety requirements. The role also involves documenting work, escalating issues when necessary, and working non-standard hours including nights and weekends. Ideal candidates are reliable, technically inclined, and possess basic IT knowledge with a strong work ethic.

  1. Prepares, stages, sets up, and performs startups and shutdowns (e.g., racks, hard drives, switches) according to specific instructions, checklists, guides, standard protocols, and emails.
  2. Performs post-execution quality checks and verifies that grounding, staging, labeling, and cabling are set up properly according to safety protocols, deployment standards, and planned Network Design Tasks (NDTs).
  3. Performs diagnostics and troubleshooting following standard procedures, quickly identifies the cause(s) of issues, and replaces faulty components
  4. Decommissions hardware for simple changes and refreshes (e.g., memory upgrades, OS rebuilds) following standard procedures with minimal guidance
  5. Follows procedures to communicate, report, and escalate incidents to appropriate Datacenter Technician Leads

Job Summary:

A proactive and detail-oriented Embedded Software Engineer to join our team building cutting-edge unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) systems. You will contribute to the design, development, and deployment of both embedded flight software and ground control station (GCS) software that together enable intelligent and autonomous flight.

This role is well-suited to engineers with a solid foundation in embedded systems and 2+ years of experience who are eager to expand their impact. You’ll collaborate closely with hardware, systems, and software teams on projects spanning flight control, communication, navigation, and operator interfaces. We value clean, maintainable code and a growth mindset—engineers who are curious, collaborative, and committed to learning.



Primary Responsibilities:


1. Software Development (~55%)

· Design, implement, and maintain embedded software for UAV flight platforms

· Contribute to the development of Ground Control Station (GCS) software, including user interfaces, telemetry, and mission tools

· Write reliable, maintainable, and well-documented code in C++, C, Python and/or MATLAB

· Develop and support real-time flight software, including control loops, sensor drivers, and mission logic

· Implement and test communication protocols such as UART, CAN, TCP/IP, UDP, and serial

· Design, develop and support software tools for UAV system configuration

· Develop software modules and system services targeting embedded Linux platforms, including process management, device interfaces, and inter-process communication


2. Testing & Integration (~20%)

· Support testing and debugging in both lab environments and during field flight tests

· Collaborate with hardware, electrical, and systems engineers to bring up and integrate new UAV platforms


3. Documentation & Architecture (~10%)

· Document internal designs, software architecture, and interfaces to support maintainability and team alignment


4. Support & Maintenance (~10%)

· Maintain and support existing embedded and ground control software

· Support software tools for UAV system configuration


5. Professional Development (~5%)

· Stay current with UAV industry developments and continuously grow your skillset

· Collaborating with other software developers

· Regularly report on activities and progress

The Department of Natural Resources (DNR) is seeking a detail-oriented and proactive cybersecurity professional to join our Information Technology Division as a Cybersecurity Analyst. In this vital role, you’ll take point on threat hunting, endpoint detect and response administration and supporting the DNR IT Cybersecurity program as a first line of defense and customer contact for security operations, handling alerts, and assisting our scientists, server operators, and law enforcement officers with security related issues, and escalating complex incidents to senior staff. You’ll work closely with other cybersecurity staff daily to participate in vulnerability management, attack surface management, governance, and with engineers and architects to ensure DNR people, data, and systems maintain confidentiality, integrity, and availability to serve the residents of the great state of Washington. Your work will have a direct impact on DNR's mission-critical services across Washington state. If you're ready to grow your career in public sector cybersecurity and join a highly collaborative environment, apply today and help secure Washington's natural resource systems for the future.Responsibilities:
• Assess and respond to security incidents or risks, gather needed information, and find appropriate solutions.
• Use multiple centralized endpoint protection tools to monitor client workstations. Resolve issues in endpoint detection and response client software by working directly with end users.
• Assist in the analysis of and response to cybersecurity incidents, including initial forensic analysis.
• Work closely with service desk, desktop engineering, and cybersecurity staff to coordinate efforts to protect and secure data, systems, our users, and the business.
• Assist others in configuration and maintenance of Microsoft 365 endpoint technologies as needed.
• Engage with employees after security incidents to provide personalized consultations on how to prevent future incidents.
• Educate employees on the importance of cybersecurity, explaining best practices, policies, and procedures in simple terms.
• Monitor and respond to cybersecurity technology monitoring systems, resolving issues and escalating to appropriate staff as needed
• Assist in research planning and metrics gathering for new and existing systems, helping to evaluate effectiveness and areas for improvement.
• Stay current on emerging cybersecurity trends and technologies, contributing suggestions for potential implementations.
Required Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or other closely related field AND 1 year of professional experience with Windows desktop operating systems;
OR an equivalent combination of education and/or experience.
• Experience with Windows Server, including Remote Desktop, built-in system logs, and monitoring tools.
• Experience with a centrally managed malware protection solution in an enterprise environment.
• Experience with Azure / Microsoft 365 / Defender for Endpoint / Office 365 cloud computing administration and configuration.
• Knowledge of:
• General cybersecurity and information systems concepts and principles.
• Documenting information security procedures.
• Mainstream operating systems and a wide range of information technologies, such as network, server, ITSM systems, automated policy compliance tools, and desktop productivity software.
• Commitment to fostering and supporting an environment that honors diversity, equity, inclusion, and environmental justice practices.
• Perform assigned duties in a manner consistent with applicable laws, regulations, and public records retention and requests are followed.
• DNR Core Competencies:
• Personal Accountability
• Value Others
• Compassionate Communication
• Inspire Others
• Commitment to Excellence

Special Requirements and Conditions of Employment:
• Must pass background check and fingerprinting check due to occasional exposure to and work with Criminal Justice Information, law enforcement data, and sensitive data.
• Office environment: The assigned Duty station for the position is the NRB in Olympia, WA. This position is eligible for telework options with prior approval from the direct supervisor.
• Occasional travel for trainings and/or meetings.
Desired Qualifications:
• Two (2) years of professional experience managing Windows Server in an enterprise at an intermediate level.
• Quantitative analytics experience.
• PowerShell scripting experience.
• Sec+, Network+, MCITP, MCSE, MCSA certifications.

At DNR, we approach our work through the lens of making Washington a better place environmentally and are dedicated to serving Washington’s lands and communities through diversity, equity, and inclusion.
Our Vision – Our actions ensure a future where Washington’s lands, waters, and communities thrive.
Our Mission – Manage, sustain, and protect the health and productivity of Washington’s lands and waters to meet the needs of present and future generations.

Overview

At PNNL, our core capabilities are divided among major departments that we refer to as Directorates within the Lab, focused on a specific area of scientific research or other function, with its own leadership team and dedicated budget.

Our Science & Technology directorates include National Security, Earth and Biological Sciences, Physical and Computational Sciences, and Energy and Environment. In addition, we have an Environmental Molecular Sciences Laboratory, a Department of Energy, Office of Science user facility housed on the PNNL campus.

The National Security Directorate (NSD) drives science-based, mission-focused solutions to take on complex,?real-world?threats to our nation and the world.

The AI and Data Analytics Division, part of the National Security Directorate, combines profound domain expertise and creative integration of advanced hardware and software to deliver computational solutions that address complex data and analytic challenges. Working in multidisciplinary teams, we connect foundational research to engineering to operations, providing the tools to innovate quickly and field results faster. Our strengths are integrated across the data analytics lifecycle, from data acquisition and management to analysis and decision support.

Responsibilities

We are seeking a Level 1 Software Engineer with experience in integrating specialized hardware and software using Open Architecture design approaches, notably within transportation security systems. The ideal candidate will possess experience with the Open Platform Software Library (OPSL), automation of complex workflows, and collaboration within multidisciplinary teams.

Key Responsibilities:
• Integration: Contribute to the integration of specialized security screening hardware (e.g., PNNL Developed HD-AIT, Analogic CT, Sandia developed ATR) with analytic tools using Open Architecture standards.
• OPSL SDK: Utilize the OPSL SDK capabilities to automate complex workflows and demonstrate ability to work in complex technical environments.
• Development and Implementation: Collaborate on developing new detection algorithms, user interfaces, and reporting systems. Support the creation of simulations based on generative AI models for airport passenger flow.
• Standardization Compliance: Apply understanding of Digital Imaging and Communications Security (DICOS) standards to ensure the integration and connectivity of transportation security systems.
• Collaboration & Leadership: Participate actively in team projects, design sessions, architectural reviews, and pair programming.
• Initiative and Continuous Improvement: Proactively tackle ambiguous and complex integration tasks, working with oversight from senior engineers to ensure high-quality, collaborative solutions. Provide and seek constructive feedback to drive personal and team growth through PR reviews and pair programming sessions.

Technical Skills Required:
• Familiarity with Open Architecture design principles
• Experience in OPSL
• Strong programming skills in C++ and Python
• Understanding of database management concepts using PostGres SQL
• Familiarity with Open Search
• Knowledge of AWS Native Services
• Experience in API Development and Data Engineering
• Knowledge of DevOps and DevSecOps methodologies

This position is based at the PNNL main campus in Richland, WA and requires onsite work.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:
• BS/BA or higher

Preferred Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field preferred
• Proven track record of successfully executing complex integrations in a technical environment with oversight from senior engineers
• Strong communication and collaborative skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams
• National Lab Experience

Hazardous Working Conditions/Environment

Not applicable.

Additional Information

This position requires the ability to obtain and maintain a federal security clearance.

Requirements:
• U.S. Citizenship
• Background Investigation: Applicants selected will be subject to a Federal background investigation and must meet eligibility requirements for access to classified matter in accordance with 10 CFR 710, Appendix B.
• Drug Testing: All Security Clearance positions are Testing Designated Positions, which means that the candidate selected is subject to pre-employment and random drug testing. In addition, applicants must be able to demonstrate non-use of illegal drugs, including marijuana, for the 12 consecutive months preceding completion of the requisite Questionnaire for National Security Positions (QNSP).

Note: Applicants will be considered ineligible for security clearance processing by the U.S. Department of Energy until non-use of illegal drugs, including marijuana, for 12 months can be demonstrated.

Testing Designated Position

This position is a Testing Designated Position (TDP). The candidate selected for this position will be subject to pre-employment and random drug testing for illegal drugs, including marijuana, consistent with the Controlled Substances Act and the PNNL Workplace Substance Abuse Program.

About PNNL

Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL) is a world-class research institution powered by a highly educated, diverse workforce committed to the values of Integrity, Creativity, Collaboration, Impact, and Courage. Every year, scores of dynamic, driven people come to PNNL to work with renowned researchers on meaningful science, innovations and outcomes for the U.S. Department of Energy and other sponsors; here is your chance to be one of them!

At PNNL, you will find an exciting research environment and excellent benefits including health insurance, and flexible work schedules. PNNL is located in eastern Washington State—the dry side of Washington known for its stellar outdoor recreation and affordable cost of living. The Lab’s campus is only a 45-minute flight (or ~3 hour drive) from Seattle or Portland, and is serviced by the convenient PSC airport, connected to 8 major hubs.

Commitment to Excellence and Equal Employment Opportunity

Our laboratory is committed to fostering a work environment where all individuals are treated with fairness and respect while solving critical challenges in fundamental sciences, national security, and energy resiliency. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity employer.

Pacific Northwest National Laboratory considers all applicants for employment without regard to race, religion, color, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), protected veteran status, and any other status or characteristic protected by federal, state, and/or local laws.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures and in employment. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, contact us at careers@pnnl.gov.

Drug Free Workplace

PNNL is committed to a drug-free workplace supported by Workplace Substance Abuse Program (WSAP) and complies with federal laws prohibiting the possession and use of illegal drugs.

If you are offered employment at PNNL, you must pass a drug test prior to commencing employment. PNNL complies with federal law regarding illegal drug use. Under federal law, marijuana remains an illegal drug. If you test positive for any illegal controlled substance, including marijuana, your offer of employment will be withdrawn.

Security, Credentialing, and Eligibility Requirements

In accordance with Homeland Security Presidential Directive 12 (HSPD-12) and Department of Energy (DOE) Order 473.1A, new employees are required to obtain and maintain a HSPD-12 Personal Identity Verification (PIV) Credential. To obtain this credential, new employees must successfully complete and pass a Federal Tier 1 background check investigation. This investigation includes a declaration of illegal drug activities, including use, supply, possession, or manufacture within the last year. This includes marijuana and cannabis derivatives, which are still considered illegal under federal law, regardless of state laws.

For foreign national candidates:

If you have not resided in the U.S. for three consecutive years, you are not eligible for the PIV credential and instead will need to obtain a favorable Local Site Specific Only (LSSO)risk determination to maintain employment. Once you meet the three-year residency requirement, you will be required to obtain a PIV credential to maintain employment.

Mandatory Requirements

Please be aware that the Department of Energy (DOE) prohibits DOE employees and contractors from having any affiliation with the foreign government of a country DOE has identified as a “country of risk” without explicit approval by DOE and Battelle. If you are offered a position at PNNL and currently have any affiliation with the government of one of these countries, you will be required to disclose this information and recuse yourself of that affiliation or receive approval from DOE and Battelle prior to your first day of employment.

Rockstar Rewards

Employees and their families are offered medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, robust telehealth care options, several mental health benefits, free wellness coaching, health savings account, flexible spending accounts, basic life insurance, disability insurance*, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, tuition assistance, relocation, backup childcare, legal benefits, supplemental parental bonding leave, surrogacy and adoption assistance, and fertility support. Employees are automatically enrolled in our company-funded pension plan* and may enroll in our 401 (k) savings plan with company match*. Employees may accrue up to 120 vacation hours per year and may receive ten paid holidays per year.
• Research Associates excluded.
• *All benefits are dependent upon eligibility.

Notice to Applicants

PNNL lists the full pay range for the position in the job posting. Starting pay is calculated from the minimum of the pay range and actual placement in the range is determined based on an individual’s relevant job-related skills, qualifications, and experience. This approach is applicable to all positions, with the exception of positions governed by collective bargaining agreements and certain limited-term positions which have specific pay rules.

As part of our commitment to fair compensation practices, we do not ask for or consider current or past salaries in making compensation offers at hire. Instead, our compensation offers are determined by the specific requirements of the position, prevailing market trends, applicable collective bargaining agreements, pay equity for the position type, and individual qualifications and skills relevant to the performance of the position.

Position: IT Quality Assurance Tester (Entry)

Overview

We are currently recruiting a data driven Quality Assurance Tester for our DRIVES/PRFT Production Support team.

As one of the most visible state agencies and the second-largest revenue generator in Washington State, we engage with over 6 million residents each year and collect nearly $3 billion in taxes and fees. We understand that our services are vital to the daily lives of our customers, enabling them to live, work, drive, and thrive. We take this responsibility to heart and are dedicated to providing equitable and meaningful access to our services for every resident.

Lean certification (Yellow or Green Belt) preferred.

job_title: Entry Level Business Foundations Opportunity at Year Up United Bothell, WA
job_description:
Entry Level Business Foundations Opportunity job at Year Up United. Bothell, WA.

Entry Level Business Foundations Opportunity at Year Up United summary:

Entry Level Business Foundations Opportunity through Year Up United is a one-year intensive training program providing young adults with professional and technical skills, internships, and job placement support. Participants gain knowledge in areas such as business operations, IT support, project management, and data analytics while receiving an educational stipend. The program aims to prepare graduates for successful careers with leading employers and offers ongoing coaching and mentorship.

Year Up United is a one-year or less, intensive job training program that provides young adults with in-classroom skill development, access to internships and/or job placement services, and personalized coaching and mentorship. Year Up United participants also receive an educational stipend.

The program combines technical and professional training with access to internships and job placement support through our industry-leading talent placement firm YUPRO Placement. If you receive an internship, it may be at Microsoft, Accenture, Salesforce, or Seattle Children's Hospital among other leading organizations in the Seattle/Bellevue Washington area.

Are you eligible?
You can apply to Year Up United if you are:
- A high school graduate or GED recipient
- Eligible to work in the U.S.
- Available Monday-Friday throughout the duration of the program
- Highly motivated to learn technical and professional skills
- Have not obtained a BachelorÊ¼s degree
- You may be required to answer additional screening questions when applying

What will you gain?
Professional business and communication skills, interviewing and networking skills, resume building, ongoing support and guidance to help you launch your career. During the internship phase, Year Up United students earn an educational stipend of $525 per week.

In-depth classes include:
- Application Development
- Banking
- Business Operations
- IT Support
- Project Management
- Data Analytics
- Network Security & Support

Get the skills and opportunity you need to launch your professional career.
75% of Year Up United graduates are employed and/or enrolled in postsecondary education within 4 months of graduation. Employed graduates earn an average starting salary of fifty-three thousand dollars per year.
PandoLogic. Category:General, Location:Bothell, WA-98011

Keywords:

entry level business, professional training, internship program, job placement, technical skills development, business operations, IT support, project management, data analytics, career coaching

Job Description

Please note:
• Applying for positions in multiple locations will result in a longer vetting process.
• The Whiting-Turner Contracting Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including the disabled and veterans.

Whiting-Turner Entry-Level Engineers are team members who help manage all aspects of construction projects. Reporting to a Project Manager and/or a Superintendent, the Entry-Level Engineer's major duties include:
• Coordinating work with project owner representatives, architects, engineers, inspectors, and site subcontractors and/or directing the work of all trades assigned to a construction work site.
• Managing estimating and bidding processes and tracking costs to manage project budget.
• Resolving and clarifying design issues.
• Identifying and resolving field issues and change orders.
• Writing contracts and purchase orders using company boilerplate language to document all ordered work, schedules, projected costs, change orders and technical specifications.
• Participating in meetings to coordinate work and manage labor concerns.
• Ensuring work is completed in accordance with quality standards and contract specifications.
• Implementing project schedules including each task associated with project completion.
• Documenting the technical, financial and personnel aspects of each project.
• Reviewing project drawings, specifications, submittals and change orders.
• Reviewing each trade's work progress with respect to each project phase.
• Providing field support to construction superintendents to help ensure contract compliance, quality control and maintenance of the site.
• Tracking project phase completion against milestones.
• Setting expectations for managing a safe work site.
• Documenting each phase of the project.

Required Skills

Whiting-Turner will train qualified candidates to develop:
• General knowledge of safety regulations, ANSI standards, local and state building codes, environmental codes, availability of industry-specific codes and guidelines.
• General knowledge of general construction processes, practices and work sites.
• Experience with construction projects, mechanical/electrical systems, building materials, building mechanics or working with craft workers.
• Ability to read and understand technical specifications, blueprints, technical manuals, product/material installation instructions and engineering/architectural drawings.
• Proficiency in scheduling including related scheduling software.

Required Experience

Basic qualifications for Entry-Level Engineer candidates include:
• Bachelor of Science in an Engineering discipline, Engineering Technology or Construction Management, Construction Technology.
• Knowledge of engineering principles.
• Ability to communicate effectively verbally and in writing.

and budget-compliant project completion, addressing team challenges, managing headcount 
for multiple concurrent projects, updating client status trackers, serving as the onsite point of

Brooksource is seeking a qualified and motivated individual to oversee the operation and maintenance of electrical and mechanical infrastructure within our client's Data Centers (DC). As a key member of the Engineering and Facilities team, the successful candidate will play a crucial role in maintaining the world-class mechanical and electrical infrastructure, ensuring facility operations, and implementing safety procedures.


Responsibilities

Safety:

  1. Uphold and promote the highest safety standards, fostering a world-class safety culture.
  2. Collaborate with other teams to address safety risks/issues and take ownership of safety initiatives and projects.

Engineering & Facility Operations and Maintenance:

  1. Demonstrate operational expertise in electrical and mechanical equipment, including UPS’s, Switchgear, Circuit Breakers, ATS's, Diesel Generators, Chillers, HVAC, Exhaust Fans, VFD's, and Transformers.
  2. Troubleshoot events within internal SLAs and work varying shifts (morning, late, and night).
  3. Conduct routine rounds/log-taking for operational readings of mechanical and electrical equipment.
  4. Supervise contractors performing servicing or preventive maintenance.
  5. Respond to off-duty emergency calls on an on-call rotation basis.
  6. Lift/move objects/materials within regulatory weight limits and work in outdoor weather conditions occasionally.


Basic Qualifications

  1. Completed apprenticeship as an Electronics Technician/Electrician or equivalent recognized training.
  2. Very good command of English (CEFR B2).

job_title: AI Intern – Reasoning Models & Real-Time Communication
job_description:
Duration: Part-time during academic term; Full-time over summer

Location: Remote/Hybrid (UW CoMotion @ Start Up Hall)


About the Internship:

Captus.ai is looking for a motivated AI Intern to join our team and focus on improving reasoning models and near real-time communication between AI agents and users. In this role, you will work on AI architecture, helping to enhance how AI systems think and interact. This internship is **unpaid** but offers flexible dates and scheduling, allowing you to contribute part-time during the school year and full-time over the summer. It’s a great opportunity to apply your knowledge in AI and machine learning to real-world projects, collaborate with experienced engineers, and help shape the future of interactive AI systems.


Responsibilities:

Documentation & Architecture: Review, maintain, and update documentation related to the AI architecture to ensure clarity and accuracy.

Model Improvement: Propose and implement enhancements in AI reasoning models and workflows to improve decision-making and efficiency.

Prompt Management: Manage and refine initialization prompts for AI agents, optimizing how agents are prompted to achieve better responses.

Performance Optimization: Monitor AI agent performance and cost-efficiency, identifying bottlenecks and optimizing for speed and resource usage.

Tool Development: Develop tools or scripts to track and analyze AI agent interactions, using the insights to improve efficiency and response quality.

Model Deployment: Assist in deploying and maintaining AI models using OpenAI APIs, ensuring models are up-to-date and functioning correctly in production.

Cloud Integration: Work with Render.com (and similar cloud platforms) to deploy AI services and ensure seamless integration with our applications.

Experimentation: Conduct experiments and evaluations on AI agents to test improvements, aiming to boost response accuracy and reduce latency.

Research & Innovation: Continuously research emerging AI technologies, frameworks, and best practices to recommend and implement upgrades that enhance system efficiency and capabilities.

Debugging: Debug and troubleshoot issues within AI workflows and architecture, ensuring reliable AI agent operations.

Desired Qualifications:

Education: Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field.

Programming Skills: Proficiency in Python and familiarity with AI/ML libraries or frameworks.

OpenAI & ML Tools: Experience or coursework using OpenAI APIs and knowledge of AI model fine-tuning techniques.

Cloud Deployment: Familiarity with cloud platforms (especially Render.com) for deploying applications or AI models is a plus

Analytical Ability: Ability to analyze model performance metrics and optimize for better accuracy and efficiency.

Problem-Solving: A proactive, problem-solving mindset with keen attention to detail in debugging and optimizing systems.

Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills for effective documentation and reporting on project progress and findings.

Preferred Qualifications:

Vector Databases: Experience with vector databases and knowledge retrieval systems for storing and querying AI knowledge bases.

Reinforcement Learning: Understanding of reinforcement learning techniques and how they can be applied to improve agent behavior.

AI Orchestration: Exposure to frameworks like LangChain or similar tools for orchestrating complex AI agent interactions.

Hands-on Experience: Previous experience in AI model deployment, testing, or participating in AI/ML projects (through coursework, personal projects, or prior internships).


Why Join Us?

We are a stealth startup. You will gain hands-on experience with advanced AI systems and contribute to projects pushing real-time AI communication's boundaries. You’ll work in a collaborative environment with industry professionals who are passionate about AI. This flexible internship will not only allow you to grow your technical skills but also give you an opportunity to make a meaningful impact.

Hobbes Heath, a start-up company located in Bellevue, is seeking a UX Designer to join our team and support the creation of user-friendly, engaging digital experiences. In this role, you will collaborate with designers, developers, and stakeholders to conduct user research, create wireframes and prototypes, and improve the overall usability of our products. This internship is an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in UX design while contributing to real-world projects.



Key Responsibilities:

  1. Assist in user research, including interviews, surveys, and usability testing.
  2. Create wireframes, mockups, and interactive prototypes.
  3. Collaborate with the design and development teams to refine user interfaces.
  4. Analyze user behavior and provide recommendations for improvement.

About the Role


We’re looking for an enthusiastic, proactive and technically inclined Intern to join our team at AI House. AI House is the premier AI space in Seattle for experts, entrepreneurs, community leaders, and builders who are creating the next era of AI in Seattle.

This isn’t your average intern role — you’ll be our go-to generalist, supporting our IT infrastructure and the daily rhythm of the office, helping keep everything from conference rooms booking software to coffee machines running smoothly.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced, creative, and ever-evolving environment — where no two days look the same and where you’re empowered to build and optimize at scale.


Key Responsibilities

  1. Serve as a jack-of-all-trades for IT and facilities support, wearing multiple hats throughout the day
  2. Troubleshoot and maintain office and AV tech systems (e.g., check-in platforms, conference room AV, conference room booking software etc.
  3. Optimize and scale office systems by identifying inefficiencies and proactively implementing improvements—leveraging existing tools, introducing automation, and applying AI
  4. Support general office operations, including light cleaning, organization, restocking supplies, and ensuring shared spaces are tidy and welcoming
  5. Assist with event setup and teardown, AV testing, and guest hospitality


What We're Looking For


Minimum Requirements:

  1. Current technology program student or recent graduate.
  2. Availability to work 10–15 hours per week on-site
  3. Experience in IT and tech-enabled workflows
  4. Strong interest in and experience with AI and vibe coding, the process of quickly creating, adapting, or hacking together functional digital solutions
  5. Tech-savvy and curious about how systems work (and how they break)
  6. Ability to lift or move light equipment (e.g., chairs, cables, tech gear) when needed

Preferred Qualifications

  1. Currently pursuing a degree or certification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related technical field (We welcome applicants from diverse academic backgrounds who can demonstrate strong technical aptitude and a willingness to learn).

You’ll Excel If You:

  1. Thrive in fast-moving startup environments and bring calm and clarity to the table.
  2. Are comfortable with ambiguity and shifting priorities.
  3. Take initiative and act proactively — you don’t wait to be told when you see a problem that needs solving.
  4. Can communicate clearly and professionally with both technical and non-technical team members.
  5. Have experience using or building with low-code/no-code tools, and you're eager to test and integrate new tech.
  6. Love to experiment with ways to make processes faster, simpler, and more delightful.
  7. Aren’t afraid to roll up your sleeves, whether it's solving a connectivity issue or sweeping up after an event.

How to Apply


Send an email to leslie@ai2incubator.com including a link to your LinkedIn profile, your resume, and answers to the questions below:

  1. Why this role speaks to you
  2. Your favorite tech tool or automation
  3. One system you’ve helped build or improve — large or small

Role Overview

As an IT Field Technician, you will be responsible for performing on-site and remote technical support for clients. This role is ideal for someone starting an IT career who is eager to learn and grow in networking, systems administration, and cloud technologies.


You will handle workstation setups, troubleshooting for our break/fix clients, and perform monthly client network maintenance.

You’ll receive mentorship and training, with opportunities to advance into more specialized roles within Firefly.


Key Responsibilities

  1. Workstation Setups: Configure, deploy, and troubleshoot workstations for clients, ensuring they meet company standards and client-specific needs.
  2. Break/Fix Support: Diagnose and resolve hardware and software issues for our break/fix clients on time.
  3. Monthly Maintenance: Perform scheduled maintenance tasks, including system updates, security checks, and network health assessments.
  4. Documentation: Maintain accurate records of tasks, configurations, and troubleshooting steps in the company’s documentation platform. Client Support: Provide excellent customer service, both on-site and remotely, ensuring client satisfaction and timely resolution of issues.
  5. Collaboration: Work closely with account managers, account technicians, and the broader team to deliver seamless service to our clients. Learning & Growth: Actively engage in training opportunities to expand your knowledge in networking, systems administration, and cloud administration.

About WSDOT

The Washington State Department of Transportation (WSDOT) is a multimodal agency with a global reputation for excellence. Our dedicated workforce plans, designs, builds, and operates an integrated transportation system that safely and efficiently moves people and goods throughout the state. In addition to maintaining over 20,000 lane miles of state highway and 4,100 bridges, WSDOT manages the world's longest floating bridge, leads an award-winning Active Transportation Plan, holds the record for the world’s widest tunneling project, and operates the largest ferry system in the nation!

The Opportunity

WSDOT is seeking a skilled and motivated systems administration and cybersecurity professional to join our team in the Olympic & Southwest Regions. As a regional technician and cybersecurity specialist, you will be at the forefront of protecting WSDOT’s critical systems and infrastructure. In this journey-level position, you will apply advanced technical expertise across a broad spectrum of cybersecurity and systems administration disciplines. You’ll lead the charge in assessing and mitigating risks, implementing effective security controls, and ensuring secure, reliable access to essential IT services. Your work will directly support 24/7 operations critical to regional transportation systems, partner agencies, and public-facing services. As the IT System Administrator – Journey, you will collaborate with project managers, technicians, and operational teams to design, implement, and manage security and system solutions across cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. Your guidance will be instrumental in developing standards, conducting security assessments, and maintaining resilient infrastructure capable of withstanding evolving cyber threats. If you're passionate about cybersecurity, systems management, and making a real-world impact on public safety and transportation, this is the role for you.

What to Expect

The Olympic-Southwest Technology Services (OSW TS) team provides consultation, analysis, planning, design, development, testing, configuration, implementation, operation, administration, maintenance, troubleshooting and asset management of multi-regional servers, virtual environments, network infrastructure, telecommunications systems, software, reproduction, operating systems, workstations and mobile devices. OSW TS provides these services to over 1400 employees and 2382 IT end devices remotely and at 54 sites within 13 counties for two distinct business regions, Olympic and Southwest, enabling WSDOT employees to complete their assigned work activities in support of the agency’s mission.

Among the varied range of responsibilities held within this role, the IT System Administrator – Journey will:
• Design, implement, maintain, troubleshoot and update virtual and cloud infrastructure.
• Plan, implement and lead deployment projects of new virtual infrastructure and provide expert consultation to all IT staff to ensure success of projects, positive customer experience, and educational/training opportunities to staff.
• Support all software, hardware, and networking aspects of Active Directory (AD), Cloud services, servers, applications, database management systems, replication, archiving and security.
• Independently monitor Application, Database & Operating System event logs as well as server performance and network infrastructure to analyze trends and identify issues.
• Develop plans and remediate incidents based on security audits and/or evaluations for the OSW Regions.
• Provide project management of tasks related to cybersecurity, to ensure timely mitigation and remediation of risk, and maintain standards and policy compliance.
• Conduct technical research and testing of operating systems, applications, database management systems, storage, backups and replication systems.
• Determine viability of increased levels of virtualization and ensure disaster recovery reclamation in support of Agency virtualization and business continuity missions.

Qualifications

To be considered for this opportunity, at least one (1) of the following are required:
• Eight (8) years of IT experience working in a customer serviced-focused technology organization. OR
• An associate’s degree in computer science or closely related field and six (6) years of experience working in Information Technology. OR
• A bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science or Management Information Systems or Software Development, or related field and four (4) years of experience working in Information Technology.

In addition to one (1) of the above, the following are also required:
• Two (2) years of experience that demonstrates system administration troubleshooting skills and problem solving with an ability to make independent decisions and judgements about workflows and work priorities.
• Experience providing technical consulting and performing installation of IT hardware and software.
• Effective communication skills with the ability to clearly communicate professionally with a diverse set of peers and vendors in written and verbal form.
• Skills and the ability comprehend the security implications of vulnerabilities and apply mitigation strategies.
• Skills to provide technical guidance and advice to peer IT staff.
• A valid driver’s license.
• Knowledge of:
• Advanced diagnostic tools: HP Systems Insight Manager, Solar Winds Engineers Toolset, SQL Admin Studio and Fluke Optiview
• Active Directory Users & Computers, Exchange Management Console and PowerShell
• Server, PC, peripherals, hardware, software, architecture, and operational theory.
• Microsoft Operating systems, applications, management and technical tools.
• Imaging, group policies, and task sequence
• Networked computing environment and infrastructure

It is preferred that qualified candidates also have:
• One (1) year of experience in cybersecurity within at least two (2) the following cybersecurity technologies:
• Vulnerability management
• Intrusion detection and prevention
• Application security
• Network security
• Incident response and handling
• Anti-virus/malware management
• Security assessments

Why WSDOT

How to Apply

This is a Washington National Guard Title 5 Excepted Service Technician position:

IT CYBERSECURITY SPECIALIST (INFOSEC)

Location: 262 Cyberspace Operations Squadron

Joint Base Lewis-McChord, Washington 98438

Air National Guard Units

Appointment Type: Permanent, excepted service

Advertised as: Title 5

Announcement may be used to fill one or more vacancies.

Your resume must contain your own words. Resumes that are verbatim, from this announcement, will not be considered.

Overview

The Computing and Information Technology Directorate works to re-imagine how digital technologies can simplify the research experience, enable data-driven insights for decision makers, and accelerate PNNL’s research missions. CIT helps connect staff to systems while enabling a broader research computing ecosystem to improve productivity and provide a sense of community.

Responsibilities

Designs, develops, documents, tests, and debugs new and existing software systems, hardware/software interfaces, and/or applications according to industry established software engineering principals and best practices. Works collaboratively within a team to execute on the full system development lifecycle including analyzing user needs to determine technical requirements; developing technical specifications based on conceptual design and requirements; developing well-crafted and documented source code; integrating hardware using software; automating manual tasks; and consulting with the end user to prototype, configure, refine, test, and debug programs or systems to meet needs. Identifies and evaluates new technologies or methods for implementation and continuous improvement.

Applies engineering and technical principles to the design and development of computer networks, cyber security infrastructure, telecommunications, information technology systems and tools, and database systems. Analyzes, installs, integrates, modifies, and supports all associated systems, networks, software, and databases. Plans and conducts projects after analysis of functional and operational requirements. May consult with and lead cross-functional teams.

Key Responsibilities
• Design, develop, test, and maintain scalable software solutions using a variety of programming languages (e.g., Python, JavaScript, C#, etc.).
• Build and manage CI/CD pipelines for efficient code deployment using tools like GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, or similar.
• Develop and manage APIs using Azure API Management (APIM) and deploy applications in Azure App Service Environments (ASE).
• Create low-code/no-code solutions using Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate (formerly Power Flow), and Copilot Studio.
• Contribute to AI-driven application development and integration of intelligent automation features.
• Advocate for and implement automation best practices across development and operational workflows.
• Take initiative in leading small-to-medium projects or product features in an agile/scrum environment.
• Collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders, UX designers, business analysts, and cloud infrastructure teams.
• Ensure security, compliance, and performance optimization are integral to all development efforts.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:
• BS/BA or higher

Preferred Qualifications:
• Microsoft certifications (e.g., PL-400, AZ-204, AZ-400).
• Experience with AI integration, data workflows, or machine learning in business applications.
• Familiarity with containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Bicep, Terraform).

Hazardous Working Conditions/Environment

Not Applicable

Additional Information

This position requires the ability to obtain and maintain a federal security clearance.

Requirements:
• U.S. Citizenship
• Background Investigation: Applicants selected will be subject to a Federal background investigation and must meet eligibility requirements for access to classified matter in accordance with 10 CFR 710, Appendix B.
• Drug Testing: All Security Clearance positions are Testing Designated Positions, which means that the candidate selected is subject to pre-employment and random drug testing. In addition, applicants must be able to demonstrate non-use of illegal drugs, including marijuana, for the 12 consecutive months preceding completion of the requisite Questionnaire for National Security Positions (QNSP).

Note: Applicants will be considered ineligible for security clearance processing by the U.S. Department of Energy until non-use of illegal drugs, including marijuana, for 12 months can be demonstrated.

Testing Designated Position

This position is a Testing Designated Position (TDP). The candidate selected for this position will be subject to pre-employment and random drug testing for illegal drugs, including marijuana, consistent with the Controlled Substances Act and the PNNL Workplace Substance Abuse Program.

About PNNL

Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL) is a world-class research institution powered by a highly educated, diverse workforce committed to the values of Integrity, Creativity, Collaboration, Impact, and Courage. Every year, scores of dynamic, driven people come to PNNL to work with renowned researchers on meaningful science, innovations and outcomes for the U.S. Department of Energy and other sponsors; here is your chance to be one of them!

At PNNL, you will find an exciting research environment and excellent benefits including health insurance, and flexible work schedules. PNNL is located in eastern Washington State—the dry side of Washington known for its stellar outdoor recreation and affordable cost of living. The Lab’s campus is only a 45-minute flight (or ~3 hour drive) from Seattle or Portland, and is serviced by the convenient PSC airport, connected to 8 major hubs.

Commitment to Excellence and Equal Employment Opportunity

Our laboratory is committed to fostering a work environment where all individuals are treated with fairness and respect while solving critical challenges in fundamental sciences, national security, and energy resiliency. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity employer.

Pacific Northwest National Laboratory considers all applicants for employment without regard to race, religion, color, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), protected veteran status, and any other status or characteristic protected by federal, state, and/or local laws.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures and in employment. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, contact us at careers@pnnl.gov.

Drug Free Workplace

PNNL is committed to a drug-free workplace supported by Workplace Substance Abuse Program (WSAP) and complies with federal laws prohibiting the possession and use of illegal drugs.

If you are offered employment at PNNL, you must pass a drug test prior to commencing employment. PNNL complies with federal law regarding illegal drug use. Under federal law, marijuana remains an illegal drug. If you test positive for any illegal controlled substance, including marijuana, your offer of employment will be withdrawn.

Security, Credentialing, and Eligibility Requirements

In accordance with Homeland Security Presidential Directive 12 (HSPD-12) and Department of Energy (DOE) Order 473.1A, new employees are required to obtain and maintain a HSPD-12 Personal Identity Verification (PIV) Credential. To obtain this credential, new employees must successfully complete and pass a Federal Tier 1 background check investigation. This investigation includes a declaration of illegal drug activities, including use, supply, possession, or manufacture within the last year. This includes marijuana and cannabis derivatives, which are still considered illegal under federal law, regardless of state laws.

For foreign national candidates:

If you have not resided in the U.S. for three consecutive years, you are not eligible for the PIV credential and instead will need to obtain a favorable Local Site Specific Only (LSSO)risk determination to maintain employment. Once you meet the three-year residency requirement, you will be required to obtain a PIV credential to maintain employment.

Mandatory Requirements

Please be aware that the Department of Energy (DOE) prohibits DOE employees and contractors from having any affiliation with the foreign government of a country DOE has identified as a “country of risk” without explicit approval by DOE and Battelle. If you are offered a position at PNNL and currently have any affiliation with the government of one of these countries, you will be required to disclose this information and recuse yourself of that affiliation or receive approval from DOE and Battelle prior to your first day of employment.

Rockstar Rewards

Employees and their families are offered medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, health savings account, flexible spending accounts, basic life insurance, disability insurance*, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, tuition assistance, supplemental parental bonding leave**, surrogacy and adoption assistance, and fertility support. Employees are automatically enrolled in our company funded pension plan* and may enroll in our 401k savings plan. Employees may accrue up to 120 vacation hours per year and may receive ten paid holidays per year.
• Research Associates excluded.

Notice to Applicants

PNNL lists the full pay range for the position in the job posting. Starting pay is calculated from the minimum of the pay range and actual placement in the range is determined based on an individual’s relevant job-related skills, qualifications, and experience. This approach is applicable to all positions, with the exception of positions governed by collective bargaining agreements and certain limited-term positions which have specific pay rules.

As part of our commitment to fair compensation practices, we do not ask for or consider current or past salaries in making compensation offers at hire. Instead, our compensation offers are determined by the specific requirements of the position, prevailing market trends, applicable collective bargaining agreements, pay equity for the position type, and individual qualifications and skills relevant to the performance of the position.

Minimum Salary

USD $81,300.00/Yr.
Maximum Salary

USD $119,500.00/Yr.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The Help Desk Support Technician provides technical support to staff across the museum, ensuring smooth daily operations of hardware, software, and office equipment. This part-time role is responsible for monitoring and responding to Technology Help Desk requests, troubleshooting issues, and assisting with workstation setup and maintenance. You’ll play a key role in keeping SAM’s technology running efficiently by:


  1. Assisting staff with Windows, Mac, and productivity software.
  2. Monitoring the Technology Help Desk service queue, prioritizing and escalating requests as needed.
  3. Documenting, closing, and archiving service requests, and preparing periodic Help Desk reports.
  4. Installing, configuring, and maintaining computers, peripherals, and official productivity software.
  5. Troubleshooting PC and Mac hardware and networking issues.
  6. Ensuring printers and other office equipment are in good working order, coordinating repairs as needed.
  7. Organizing obsolete hardware and coordinating disposal.
  8. Supporting account management for Active Directory, Tessitura, Microsoft 365, and other SaaS platforms.
  9. Participating in SAM’s institutional learning efforts, including equity, diversity, inclusion, accessibility, and belonging (EDIAB).


FLSA STATUS: Part Time, Non-Exempt

REPORTS TO: Director of Technology

COMPENSATION: $31.44/hour


WORK SCHEDULE

This role is scheduled for 20 hours per week during standard business hours (Monday–Friday). Occasional holiday or peak season work may be required to support museum operations.


WE ARE LOOKING FOR A customer-focused IT professional who can troubleshoot effectively, manage multiple requests, and support staff with patience and clarity. You’ll bring:


Required Qualifications

  1. High school diploma or equivalent.
  2. 2–4 years’ experience installing, configuring, and troubleshooting Microsoft Windows, PC hardware, Mac, and peripherals.
  3. 2–4 years’ experience with TCP/IP networking and wireless access protocols.
  4. Demonstrated customer service skills.
  5. Ability to prioritize and multitask in a fast-paced environment.
  6. Strong time management, problem solving, and organizational skills.
  7. Ability to work independently with discretion and confidentiality.
  8. Proficiency with Microsoft Office, Asana, Slack, and internet-based research.
  9. Ability to pass and maintain a security background check.


Preferred Qualifications

  1. Progress toward an AA or BS in Computer Science, MIS, or related field.
  2. Experience in a museum, nonprofit, or corporate environment.


WORK CONDITIONS

Exposure: Primarily indoors in a climate-controlled environment, including server rooms and front-of-house/back-of-house spaces. Stationary Work: About 75% at a desk; 25% moving around work areas. May require extended standing during events. Communication: Clear and professional verbal and written communication in English is required. Office Work: Regular use of computers, phones, and office equipment. Lifting/Carrying: Ability to transport up to 25 pounds of equipment and supplies.
minimumeducationlevel: Some training or study post high school
